Übersetzungswerkstatt

Übersetzungswerkstatt

Philosophical German Translation

0 / 0

About Translation Practice

This app focuses on translating complete philosophical texts and passages in their entirety. The individual exercises break longer works — the Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus, Das Unheimliche, and Zur Genealogie der Moral — into manageable segments for systematic translation practice.

Select an author from the tabs at the top of the sidebar, then choose a passage from the list to begin. Each passage displays the original German text — click any word to see its gloss and part of speech.

Type your English translation in the input area, then click Evaluate for AI-powered feedback on accuracy and style. Use Get Hints to reveal contextual clues, or Show Reference to compare with a published translation.

Your progress is tracked in the bar at the top of the sidebar. A passage is marked complete once evaluated. To add custom texts or reset progress, use the Settings menu.

Evaluation

Reference Translation
Source texts drawn from standard published editions. Verify against your own copies for critical scholarly work. Wittgenstein: Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus (1921); Freud: "Das Unheimliche" (1919); Nietzsche: various works; Zur Genealogie der Moral (1887). Reference translations are for study purposes and should not be treated as authoritative.

Settings

When set, translations are evaluated using LLM. Without a key, client-side heuristics are used. Your key is stored only in this browser's localStorage.

Optional. When set, OpenAI's neural TTS is used for German audio regardless of your LLM provider above — so you can use Anthropic or Gemini for evaluation while still using OpenAI for speech. If blank, falls back to the OpenAI LLM key (when provider is OpenAI) or browser voices.

For better German TTS quality, download enhanced voices in System Preferences → Accessibility → Spoken Content (macOS) or Settings → Text-to-speech (Windows).

If you have added this app to your home screen, you will need to remove it and re-add it for the new icon to take effect.

This clears which words have been automatically glossed, so all glosses will reappear on the next passage.

This clears all vocabulary tracking and exercise completion data.

Import Text

Import a JSON file with additional exercises. Expected format:

{
  "id": "my-text",
  "title": "Text Title",
  "author": "Author Name",
  "exercises": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "label": "Section 1",
      "german": "German text here...",
      "reference": "English reference here..."
    }
  ],
  "vocabulary": {
    "wort": { "pos": "NOUN", "gloss": "word" }
  }
}

Sync Progress Across Devices

Progress is stored in this browser's localStorage and does not sync automatically. Use export/import to transfer your progress between browsers or devices.

Download a JSON file containing your completion data, API key, and any imported custom texts. Transfer this file to another device and import it there.

Load a previously exported progress file. This will merge with your existing progress—exercises already marked complete on this device stay complete.

  1. On the device with your current progress, click Export Progress File.
  2. Transfer the downloaded .json file to the other device (e.g., via email, cloud storage, or USB).
  3. On the other device, open this app, open Sync Progress, and use Import Progress File to load the file.
  4. Progress is merged: any exercise completed on either device will be marked complete.

Fortschritt zurücksetzen?

Dieser Vorgang löscht Vokabel-, Grammatik- und Übungsfortschritt sowie alle FSRS-Lernkarten. Dies kann nicht rückgängig gemacht werden.
